Query: NESTROFT test is a screening test for –

Document [0] (Title: Anemia Screening -- Diagnostic Tests) Osmotic fragility test: This test evaluates whether RBCs are more fragile than usual, which may indicate hereditary spherocytosis.
Document [1] (Title: Anemia Screening -- Diagnostic Tests) Hemoglobin electrophoresis: This test evaluates hemoglobin amino acid chains, aiding in the diagnosis of hemoglobinopathies such as thalassemia or sickle cell disease.
Document [2] (Title: Anemia Screening -- Diagnostic Tests) Genetic testing: This test can confirm conditions such as hereditary spherocytosis, pyruvate kinase deficiency, or other rare hereditary anemias.
Document [3] (Title: Anemia Screening -- Diagnostic Tests) Glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase activity test: This test detects glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency, a common enzymatic cause of hemolytic anemia.

Document [9] (Title: Laboratory Evaluation of Immune Hemolytic Anemias -- Diagnostic Tests -- Osmotic Fragility Test) Osmotic fragility measures the ability of RBCs to withstand swelling and lysis in a hypotonic solution. Increased fragility is characteristic of certain types of hemolytic anemia, such as hereditary spherocytosis.
Document [10] (Title: Anemia Screening -- Diagnostic Tests) Soluble transferrin receptor: This test differentiates between IDA and anemia of chronic disease.
Document [11] (Title: Anemia Screening -- Diagnostic Tests) Urinalysis: This test evaluates for the presence of hemoglobinuria or hemosiderinuria, which may indicate intravascular hemolysis.
Document [12] (Title: Anemia Screening -- Diagnostic Tests) Peripheral blood smear: This test provides a microscopic evaluation of RBC morphology, which can aid in diagnosing structural abnormalities.

Document [16] (Title: Anemia Screening -- Diagnostic Tests) Lead levels: This test assesses lead toxicity, which can contribute to anemia through disrupted hemoglobin synthesis, particularly in children.
Document [17] (Title: Laboratory Evaluation of Beta Thalassemia -- Testing Procedures -- Supplemental Tests) Some additional tests are necessary for thalassemia patients, which can be classified as iron-related and non-iron-related. Document [31] (Title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 3 -- Community 1538) Other deficiency anemias is a broad diagnostic category that includes several specific types of anemia. It is classified as فقر الدم الناتج عن نقص الفولات (folate‑deficiency anemia) and as فقر الدم حديدي الأرومات (iron‑deficiency anemia), reflecting its inclusion of nutrient‑deficiency–related blood disorders. In addition, the category is classified as فقر دم انحلالي ليس ناجمًا عن المناعة الذاتية (non‑immune‑mediated hemolytic anemia), indicating that it also encompasses hemolytic anemias that are not caused by autoimmunity. Within this grouping, فقر الدم الانحلالي المكتسب (acquired hemolytic anemia) is identified as a child or subcategory, further specifying a type of hemolysis that develops secondary to external factors. Together, these relationships show that “Other deficiency anemias” serves as an umbrella term that integrates both deficiency‑based anemias (folate and iron) and certain non‑immune hemolytic anemias, including acquired forms, which is important for accurate diagnosis, classification, and patient management.
